column1: # OWASP ZAP baseline-scan rule suppressions for wl_manager.
# Format (tab-separated, ZAP standard):
#   <rule_id>
# Actions:
#   IGNORE  — suppress the finding entirely (treat as known-accepted)
#   WARN    — keep at WARN level (informational; doesn't fail the run)
#   FAIL    — escalate to FAIL (causes ZAP exit code != 0 and CI failure)
# Default if a rule isn't listed here: WARN.
# Workflow setting `-l FAIL` means only FAIL-level findings fail CI;
# WARN findings get reported in the HTML/Markdown artifacts.
# Maintenance rule: every IGNORE entry MUST cite (1) the rule's
# title, (2) the Splunk-specific reason it's acceptable, and (3)
# the date it was added. Don't suppress a finding just because it's
# noisy — explain WHY it's acceptable for this app.
# First-run note: this file is intentionally minimal at Ring 5 Day 3
# inception. The first nightly run will produce a baseline of ~20-30
# WARN-level findings (mostly Splunk Web UI defaults: X-Frame-Options
# value, missing CSP, anti-CSRF tokens on Splunk's own forms, etc.).
# Triage that run's report (artifact: zap-baseline-report.html),
# then either suppress here (IGNORE) or fix upstream. Update the
# CHANGELOG with the triage outcome.
# ── Suppressions (start empty; populate after first run triage) ───
# Example entry format (commented out so it doesn't take effect):

column3: X-Frame-Options not set — Splunk's own Web UI emits
ALLOWFROM by default; acceptable for our app context

column1: # Likely first-run findings (predicted; verify against actual report):
# 10020 — Missing Anti-clickjacking Header (X-Frame-Options)
#   Splunk sets X-Frame-Options ALLOWFROM on its own pages, which
#   modern browsers treat as missing. Splunk's design assumes
#   trusted intranet deployment. ACTION: likely IGNORE with a
#   pointer to Splunk's design rationale.
# 10038 — Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
#   Splunk doesn't ship a CSP. Setting one is a Splunk-bundle
#   change, not a wl_manager change. ACTION: IGNORE with note.
# 10049 — Storable but Non-Cacheable Content
#   Informational. ACTION: WARN (no action needed).
# 10063 — Permissions Policy Header Not Set
#   Splunk default. ACTION: IGNORE with note.
# 10096 — Timestamp Disclosure
#   Splunk dashboards include timestamps as part of the UX.
#   ACTION: IGNORE with note.
